LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - PLAINTIFFS JONATHAN AXELRAD AND EMILY HATAS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE POLICY OF LIBERALITY IN ALLOWING AMENDED AND SUPPLEMENTAL PLEADINGS FAVORS GRANTING THE MOTION. (E.G., FLOOD V. SIMPSON (1975) 45 CAL.APP.3D 644, 647.) DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF DELAYED UNDULY OR THAT THEY WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED, AS THE TRIAL DATE IS MAY 17, 2021 AND DISCOVERY HAS NOT YET CLOSED. DEFENDANTS MAY CHALLENGE THE VIABILITY OF THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT BY WAY OF DEMURRER OR OTHER MOTION. (SEE, E.G., CALIFORNIA CASUALTY GEN. INS. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 281 ["THE PREFERABLE PRACTICE WOULD BE TO PERMIT THE AMENDMENT AND ALLOW THE PARTIES TO TEST ITS LEGAL SUFFICIENCY BY DEMURRER, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS OR OTHER APPROPRIATE PROCEEDINGS."].) DEFENDANTS SHALL MEET AND CONFER AS REQUIRED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41 BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDING NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants And Cross-Complainants Melanie Leungs And Sequoia Real Estate, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Brooke Anderson, Esq. (415-984-8200; banderson@nixonpeabody.com) of Nixon Peabody LLP for Plaintiff.
Devin Courteau, Esq. (415-577-9271; dcourteau@grsm.com) of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP for Defendants Sequoia Real Estate, Inc. and Melanie Leung.
Karin Landry, Esq.for Defendants Clear Windows and Clear Doors, Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SEQUOIA REAL ESTATE, INC. and MELANIE LEUNG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT:

Defendants Melanie Leung and Sequoia Real Estate, Inc.'s summary judgment motion is denied.
Plaintiffs are buyers of a house. They plead that a large picture window leaks and an upstairs bathroom is not code-compliant. Leung was the house's listing agent; she works for Sequoia. Leung and Sequoia are charged with "intentional non-disclosure of a material fact/fraudulent concealment" and negligent misrepresentation.

Leung asserts that her role was "merely as real estate agent and broker" and she "had no knowledge of" the "leak in the large picture window" or the "code violation regarding the upstairs bathroom ceiling." Plaintiffs assert that Leung "acted as far more than a real estate agent," and "had deep involvement" with the sellers and contractors in the serial flipping of houses, including the one here.

As to Leung's knowledge, her texts produced in discovery show intimate knowledge of issues at the house, including specifically the picture window's leakage. (Exs. 22, 3-19, 23-27.) This raises disputed issues of material fact for trial. The fraudulent concealment count thus survives, and summary adjudication is not sought, so the negligent misrepresentation count need not be reached.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.y

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFs JONATHAN AXELRAD and EMILY HATAS' Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint is granted. The policy of liberality in allowing amended and supplemental pleadings favors granting the motion. (E.g., Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647.) Defendants have not shown that Plaintiff delayed unduly or that they will be prejudiced if the motion is granted, as the trial date is May 17, 2021 and discovery has not yet closed. Defendants may challenge the viability of the proposed amendment by way of demurrer or other motion. (See, e.g., California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) Defendants shall meet and confer as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 before filing a demurrer to the amended complaint. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceeding not reported.
